parameters.jsCode:
const source = $('Build API Request').item.json;
const results = Array.isArray($input.first().json.results) ? $input.first().json.results : [];
return results
  .filter((result) => result && (result.title || result.abstract))
  .map((result) => {
    const officialUrl = result.html_url || result.pdf_url || source.entry_url;
    const officialTitle = result.title || 'Federal Register document';
    const publicationDate = result.publication_date || null;
    return {
      json: {
        text: [
          result.title ? `Title: ${result.title}` : null,
          result.type ? `Document type: ${result.type}` : null,
          result.abstract ? `Abstract: ${result.abstract}` : null,
          result.publication_date ? `Publication date: ${result.publication_date}` : null,
          officialUrl ? `Official URL: ${officialUrl}` : null
        ].filter(Boolean).join('\n\n'),
        title: officialTitle,
        url: officialUrl,
        country: source.country,
        source: 'federal_register_api',
        authority: source.authority,
        source_type: source.source_type,
        document_type: source.document_type,
        collection: source.collection,
        citation: result.citation || null,
        citation_prefix: source.citation_prefix,
        publication_date: publicationDate,
        source_registry_id: source.id,
        raw_source_url: source.entry_url,
        official_url: officialUrl,
        official_title: officialTitle,
        jurisdiction: source.jurisdiction || source.country,
        metadata: {
          official: true,
          source_registry_id: source.id,
          authority: source.authority,
          collection: source.collection,
          document_type: source.document_type,
          citation: result.citation || null,
          citation_prefix: source.citation_prefix,
          publication_date: publicationDate,
          official_url: officialUrl,
          official_title: officialTitle,
          jurisdiction: source.jurisdiction || source.country,
          agencies: Array.isArray(result.agencies) ? result.agencies.map((agency) => agency.name).filter(Boolean) : [],
          federal_register_document_number: result.document_number || null,
          federal_register_type: result.type || null,
          raw_source_url: source.entry_url,
          registry_source_of_truth: source.registry_source_of_truth || null
        }
      }
    };
  });

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ text: $json.text, title: $json.title, url: $json.url, country: $json.country, source: $json.source, authority: $json.authority, source_type: $json.source_type, document_type: $json.document_type, collection: $json.collection, citation: $json.citation, citation_prefix: $json.citation_prefix, publication_date: $json.publication_date, source_registry_id: $json.source_registry_id, raw_source_url: $json.raw_source_url, official_url: $json.official_url, official_title: $json.official_title, jurisdiction: $json.jurisdiction, metadata: $json.metadata }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ url: $json.entry_url, formats: ["markdown"], onlyMainContent: true, excludeTags: ["nav", "footer", "header", "script", "style", "aside"] }) }}

object.name: metadata
object.value: ={{ { official: true, source_registry_id: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.id, authority: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.authority, collection: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.collection, document_type: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.document_type, citation: null, citation_prefix: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.citation_prefix, publication_date: $json.data.metadata.publishedTime || null, official_url: $json.data.metadata.sourceURL || $json.data.metadata.ogUrl || $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.entry_url, official_title: $json.data.metadata.title || $json.data.metadata.ogTitle || $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.name || 'Untitled official source', jurisdiction: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.jurisdiction || $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.country, scrapeDate: new Date().toISOString(), firecrawlStatusCode: $json.data.metadata.statusCode, raw_source_url: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.entry_url, description: $json.data.metadata.description || null, registry_source_of_truth: $('Is Scrape Source?').item.json.registry_source_of_truth || null } }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ text: $json.text, title: $json.title, url: $json.url, country: $json.country, source: $json.source, authority: $json.authority, source_type: $json.source_type, document_type: $json.document_type, collection: $json.collection, citation: $json.citation, citation_prefix: $json.citation_prefix, publication_date: $json.publication_date, source_registry_id: $json.source_registry_id, raw_source_url: $json.raw_source_url, official_url: $json.official_url, official_title: $json.official_title, jurisdiction: $json.jurisdiction, metadata: $json.metadata }) }}

string.name: result
string.value: =Skipped bulk source {{ $json.name }}. Bulk feeds stay in the curated registry but require a dedicated downloader/parser outside this v1 workflow.
